text:
Once upon an age, in a land rich with forests and fertile plains, there lived a young farmer named Leo. Leo’s life was quiet—his days filled with the gentle thrumming of cicadas, the rustling leaves of tall trees, and the occasional breeze that carried the scent of wildflowers. His garden was his pride. Here, atop the hill where the sky touched greenery, he tended potatoes and carrots.

But Leo wasn’t satisfied. For solitude had taught him stories—a legacy from travelers—and amidst the tales of dragons and heroes, one stood out most. A tale of perseverance amidst ruin.

One autumn, storms brewed unkindly. Leaves danced wild, rivers roared, and somewhere below storms crashed upon towering cliffs. The village was cut off; houses tossed aside like leaves. Trees groaned like ancient men mourning. Leo’s heart pounded with the fury of Poseidon.

For four weeks he waited. His hands shook from hunger. Birds scoured the sky, searching for
